name: Test Coverage Check
description: Tier B (agentStop): After session completes, verifies test coverage meets stated thresholds from testing-strategy.md.

then.prompt:
Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. This hook applies from Construction phase onward.

After this session, verify testing compliance:

1. TESTS EXIST: If implementation code was written, do corresponding test files exist?
2. COVERAGE THRESHOLD: If a coverage report is available, does it meet the target stated in .kiro/steering/testing-strategy.md? (typically ≥80% line, ≥70% branch)
3. TEST PATTERNS: Do test files follow the naming pattern from naming-conventions.md?
4. CRITICAL PATH COVERAGE: If domain logic or financial calculations were modified, do tests cover edge cases?

If coverage is below threshold or tests are missing, warn with GOV-CICD-002/003.
If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks, append to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl`:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "coverage-check", "trigger": "agentStop", "ruleId": "GOV-CICD-002", "ruleSeverity": "high", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{finding}"}
